query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

content.text:
Now let me understand the issue better. Looking at the code, I can see that the problem is in the `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function. When it parses an image source like `src="image#file.png"`, the `URI` class interprets `#` as a fragment delimiter, so the path becomes just `"image"` instead of `"image#file.png"`.

Let me search for any existing issues or discussions about this:

1. In `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, when it parses an image source like `src="image#file.png"`, the `URI` class interprets `#` as a URL fragment delimiter
2. This means `attrUri.path` only returns `image` instead of the full `image#file.png`
3. The incorrect path is then used to construct the file URI

The issue occurred when rendering cards in the reviewer or previewer. ## Root Cause The problem was in the `parseSourcesToFileScheme()` function in `TemplateManager.kt`. When parsing image/audio/video sources, the function was using `URI(attrValue).path` to extract the filename path. The Java `URI` class treats `#` as a URL fragment delimiter (as used in HTML anchors like `page.html#section`). For example: - Input: `src="image#file.png"` - After URI parsing: `attrValue.path` returns `"image"` (everything before the `#`) - The fragment `file.png` was lost - Result: Incorrect file path constructed ## The Fix **File**: `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` Changed line 367 from: ```kotlin val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, attrUri.path).toString() ``` To: ```kotlin // Use the full attribute value as the filename, not the parsed URI path. // This preserves special characters like # which are treated as fragment delimiters by URI. val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, attrValue).toString() ``` **Key Point**: We now use `attrValue` directly instead of `attrUri.path`, preserving the complete filename including special characters like `#`. The `checkUri.hasScheme()` is still used to skip URIs that already have a scheme (http://, file://, etc.), which is correct behavior. ## Test Changes **File**: `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerTest.kt` Modified two test cases to reflect the correct behavior: 1. **New test**: `parseSourcesToFileScheme - filename with hash is parsed correctly` - Verifies that filenames with `#` are correctly handled - Input: `` - Expected: `` - Note: The `#` is encoded as `%23` in the final URI by the `getFileUri()` function 2. **Updated test**: `parseSourcesToFileScheme - invalid sources not parsed` - Previously: Expected to NOT parse files with `#` - Now: Correctly parses and encodes both `#` and `%` characters - Input: `` - Expected: `` ## How It Works 1. The function still uses `URI(attrValue)` to check if there's a scheme (http://, file://, etc.) 2. If no scheme is present, it uses the full original attribute value (`attrValue`) directly 3. The `getFileUri()` function then properly encodes the path, including special characters like `#` 4. The result is a properly encoded file:/// URI that Android's WebView can resolve ## Backward Compatibility This fix maintains backward compatibility: - URIs with schemes (http://, https://, file://) are still skipped - Normal filenames without special characters work exactly as before - Filenames with special characters in the media directory path continue to be encoded correctly - The fix aligns with the behavior of version 2.16.5 and Anki Desktop ## Testing The fix includes unit tests that verify: 1. Perfect! Let me create a final summary of the fix:

## Summary

I have successfully fixed the bug where images with `#` (hashtag/sharp) characters in filenames were not displaying in AnkiDroid. Here's what was done:

### Root Cause
The issue was in the `parseSourcesToFileScheme()` function in `TemplateManager.kt`. When parsing image/audio/video sources, the function was using `URI(attrValue).path` to extract the filename. The Java `URI` class treats `#` as a URL fragment delimiter (like in HTML anchors `page.html#section`), causing everything after the `#` to be lost.

**Example:**
- Input: `src="image#file.png"`
- After URI parsing: `attrUri.path` returns only `"image"` (everything before `#`)
- Result: Incorrect file path, the image file wasn't found

### Changes Made

**1. File: `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`**
   - **Line 367**: Changed from `Paths.get(mediaDir, attrUri.path)` to `Paths.get(mediaDir, attrValue)`
   - Added explanatory comments about preserving special characters
   - The function now uses the full attribute value directly instead of the parsed URI path

**2. File: `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerTest.kt`**
   - Added new test: `parseSourcesToFileScheme - filename with hash is parsed correctly`
   - Updated test: `parseSourcesToFileScheme - invalid sources not parsed`
   - Both tests verify that filenames with `#` and other special characters are correctly parsed and encoded

### How the Fix Works

1. The function still uses `URI(attrValue)` to check if a scheme exists (http://, file://, etc.)
2. If no scheme is present, it uses the full original attribute value (`attrValue`) instead of the parsed `attrUri.path`
3. The `getFileUri()` function properly encodes the path, including special characters like `#` (encoded as `%23`)
4. Android's WebView can now correctly resolve the file:/// URI to load the image

### Backward Compatibility

- ✓ URIs with schemes (http://, file://) are still skipped correctly
- ✓ Normal filenames without special characters work exactly as before
- ✓ Special characters in media directory paths continue to be encoded correctly
- ✓ Aligns with behavior of AnkiDroid 2.16.5 and Anki Desktop
- ✓ No breaking changes to existing functionality

### Verification

- All existing tests pass
- New tests validate the fix for filenames with `#` characters
- No linter errors detected
- The fix is minimal and targeted, changing only what's necessary to resolve the issue

The fix is complete and ready for use!

tool_calls.input_json: {"file_path": "/project/FIX_SUMMARY.md", "content": "# Fix for Bug: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ## Issue Summary Images with `#` (hashtag/sharp) characters in their filenames were not displaying in AnkiDroid version 2.17.6, but worked correctly in version 2.16.5. The issue occurred when rendering cards in the reviewer or previewer. ## Root Cause The problem was in the `parseSourcesToFileScheme()` function in `TemplateManager.kt`. When parsing image/audio/video sources, the function was using `URI(attrValue).path` to extract the filename path. The Java `URI` class treats `#` as a URL fragment delimiter (as used in HTML anchors like `page.html#section`). For example: - Input: `src="image#file.png"` - After URI parsing: `attrValue.path` returns `"image"` (everything before the `#`) - The fragment `file.png` was lost - Result: Incorrect file path constructed ## The Fix **File**: `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` Changed line 367 from: ```kotlin val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, attrUri.path).toString() ``` To: ```kotlin // Use the full attribute value as the filename, not the parsed URI path. // This preserves special characters like # which are treated as fragment delimiters by URI. val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, attrValue).toString() ``` **Key Point**: We now use `attrValue` directly instead of `attrUri.path`, preserving the complete filename including special characters like `#`. The `checkUri.hasScheme()` is still used to skip URIs that already have a scheme (http://, file://, etc.), which is correct behavior. ## Test Changes **File**: `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerTest.kt` Modified two test cases to reflect the correct behavior: 1. **New test**: `parseSourcesToFileScheme - filename with hash is parsed correctly` - Verifies that filenames with `#` are correctly handled - Input: `` - Expected: `` - Note: The `#` is encoded as `%23` in the final URI by the `getFileUri()` function 2. **Updated test**: `parseSourcesToFileScheme - invalid sources not parsed` - Previously: Expected to NOT parse files with `#` - Now: Correctly parses and encodes both `#` and `%` characters - Input: `` - Expected: `` ## How It Works 1. The function still uses `URI(attrValue)` to check if there's a scheme (http://, file://, etc.) 2. If no scheme is present, it uses the full original attribute value (`attrValue`) directly 3. The `getFileUri()` function then properly encodes the path, including special characters like `#` 4. The result is a properly encoded file:/// URI that Android's WebView can resolve ## Backward Compatibility This fix maintains backward compatibility: - URIs with schemes (http://, https://, file://) are still skipped - Normal filenames without special characters work exactly as before - Filenames with special characters in the media directory path continue to be encoded correctly - The fix aligns with the behavior of version 2.16.5 and Anki Desktop ## Testing The fix includes unit tests that verify: 1. Filenames with `#` are correctly parsed and encoded 2. Multiple special characters in filenames are handled 3. Existing functionality for normal, HTTP, and file:// schemes remains intact 4. Special characters in media directory paths continue to work All tests pass with no linter errors. "}
